purpose: Native macOS backend (`runtime: "macos-user"`): runs the agent as a dedicated hidden macOS service user (_yolojail) under an Apple Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) profile — no container, no VM. macos_user.py builds all artifacts (dscl/dseditgroup provisioning argv, inheriting-ACL ACE strings, SBPL profile text, sudo/env-i/sandbox-exec launch argv, a generated Python bootstrap script) as pure data, then a thin macOS-only orchestrator executes them via sudo. darwin_packages.py materializes the config's `packages:` as a native aarch64-darwin nix buildEnv and hands its /nix/store bin dir to the launch PATH.

files.role: core: pure artifact builders (user provisioning cmds, ACLs, Seatbelt profile, launch argv, bootstrap script, RunPlan + invariants) + macOS-only orchestrator run_macos_user + 4 CLI commands (macos-setup/teardown/unshare/fix-permissions); 1664 lines
native darwin `packages:` materialization: nix eval (skip list) + nix build --print-out-paths of flake attr yoloDarwinPackages; pure argv/env builders + one impure materialize(); 209 lines

public_surface.name: yolo macos-setup
public_surface.detail: One-time: creates hidden user/group _yolojail (dscl, uid/gid >=600 first-free, IsHidden 1, de-staffed, home /Users/_yolojail 0750, random pw via openssl rand piped through env var YOLO_SBPW — never argv), provisions shared root /Users/Shared/yolo (chown host:_yolojail, chmod 2770, two inheriting `chmod +a` ACEs), prints readiness checks (python3 candidates, sandbox-exec, nix). Idempotent. Refuses root (euid 0 → exit 1). macOS only (exit 1 elsewhere).

public_surface.name: yolo macos-teardown
public_surface.detail: dseditgroup -d host_user, dscl -delete user+group, rm -rf home. Refuses root; macOS only. No-op message if user absent.

public_surface.name: yolo macos-unshare <workspace>
public_surface.detail: Runs bash script `find $ws -exec chmod -h -N {} +` stripping ALL ACLs from tree. Exit 1 if not a dir or script fails.

public_surface.name: yolo macos-fix-permissions [path]
public_surface.detail: Batched find/chmod +a retrofit of the 3 split ACEs (dir / file_inherit / file) onto a tree; default target = /Users/Shared/yolo; refuses paths inside a user home (home_containing). Exit 1 on failure.

public_surface.name: yolo run --dry-run (macos-user only)
public_surface.detail: Builds RunPlan purely (no nix, no exec) and prints: workspace, cname, interpreter+candidates, profile path, staged src, git identity, darwin pkgs summary, privileged sudo commands, full Seatbelt profile text, full bootstrap script text, launch argv, invariant violations. Exit 0 iff invariants clean, else 1. Works on ANY OS — this is the CI/parity gate.

public_surface.name: runtime: "macos-user"
public_surface.detail: Selects this backend; validated in config.py:663 ('podman'|'container'|'macos-user'). Also settable via YOLO_RUNTIME=macos-user. run_cmd.py branches BEFORE any container machinery.

public_surface.name: macos_log
public_surface.detail: off|user|full (default off) — gates the generated ~/.local/bin/yolo-log wrapper around /usr/bin/log installed by the bootstrap (off=stub exit 1; user=defaults `log show --last 5m --style compact`, passthrough for show/stream/collect/config/help; full=exec /usr/bin/log "$@").

public_surface.name: macos_shared_root
public_surface.detail: Documented override of /Users/Shared/yolo (any non-home path) — but NOT yet wired into config.py (revive-plan item pending). Only appears in docstrings/error text. Porting hazard: surface incomplete.

public_surface.name: packages / security.blocked_tools / mise tools / mcp_servers / mcp_presets / lsp_servers / env_sources
public_surface.detail: Consumed indirectly: packages → darwin nix materialization; others JSON-encoded into bootstrap env (YOLO_BLOCK_CONFIG, YOLO_MISE_TOOLS, YOLO_MCP_SERVERS, YOLO_MCP_PRESETS, YOLO_LSP_SERVERS) via config.py resolvers so the native backend enforces the same per-workspace surface as the container.

public_surface.name: bootstrap baked env (generated script)
public_surface.detail: Generated Python script sets os.environ before importing entrypoint: JAIL_HOME, HOME (=/Users/_yolojail), YOLO_AGENTS (json list, double-encoded via json.dumps(json.dumps())), YOLO_HOST_DIR (=workspace), plus baked YOLO_GIT_NAME/YOLO_GIT_EMAIL/YOLO_JJ* and the 5 YOLO_* config JSON vars above. Exact repr()-embedded literals — byte-level contract for parity.
public_surface.consumers: staged entrypoint package (Python) run as sandbox user

public_surface.name: launch env (env -i scrub)
public_surface.detail: launch_argv builds `sudo --login --set-home --user=_yolojail /usr/bin/env -i HOME=… USER=_yolojail SHELL=/bin/zsh PATH=<sandbox_path> [extras] /usr/bin/sandbox-exec -f <profile> -- /bin/zsh -c 'cd <ws> && exec <agent…>'`. HOME/USER/SHELL/PATH quartet is NOT caller-overridable. Extras: TERM, COLORTERM, YOLO_GIT_NAME/EMAIL (from host `git config --get`), env_sources-resolved keys, darwin env (PKG_CONFIG_PATH). PATH order: ~/.yolo-shims:~/.local/bin:~/.npm-global/bin:~/.local/share/mise/shims:~/go/bin:<darwin store bins>:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin.

public_surface.name: YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES
public_surface.detail: JSON-encoded packages list set (or popped) in nix subprocess env; flake reads it via builtins.getEnv (hence --impure). Same contract as the Linux image build.

public_surface.name: /var/yolo-jail (STATE_DIR)
public_surface.detail: Root-owned state dir. Contains: profile-<cname>.sb (Seatbelt, 0444 via sudo mkdir/tee/chmod), bootstrap-<cname>.py (0444), entrypoint/ (recursive cp -R of src/entrypoint, chmod -R a+rX, refreshed every run). cname = runtime.container_name_for_workspace(workspace) — shared naming scheme with container backend.

public_surface.name: /Users/_yolojail
public_surface.detail: Sandbox home: owner _yolojail:_yolojail, 0750; populated by bootstrap with ~/.yolo-shims, agent configs (~/.claude etc.), ~/.local/bin/yolo-log (0755+x).

public_surface.name: /Users/Shared/yolo
public_surface.detail: Neutral shared root: host_user:_yolojail, mode 2770 (setgid), carries 2 inheriting ACEs (`group:_yolojail allow <dir-rights…,directory_inherit>` and `<file-rights…,file_inherit,directory_inherit,only_inherit>`) so children inherit at create time — no per-run ACL walk. Workspaces MUST be outside any /Users/<name> home (home_containing check = plan invariant, hard refusal).

public_surface.name: Seatbelt SBPL profile
public_surface.detail: (version 1)(allow default) + ordered denies/re-allows: deny file-write* / re-allow ws+home+/tmp+/private/tmp+/var/folders+/private/var/folders+/dev; deny /Volumes reads except "/Volumes/Macintosh HD"; deny regex ^/dev/r?disk, ^/dev/bpf; deny /Users reads re-allow literal /Users, /Users/Shared + ws + home subpaths; deny /Library/Keychains; allow process-info*, sysctl-read. Paths SBPL-escaped (backslash+quote). Last-match-wins ordering is load-bearing.

public_surface.name: generated bash scripts
public_surface.detail: fix_permissions_script (find -type d/-exec chmod -h +a … {} + batched, echo progress), workspace_acl_strip_script (find -exec chmod -h -N {} +), macos_log_wrapper_script (3 modes). All returned as text, run via `bash -c` — golden-testable byte-for-byte.

public_surface.name: run_macos_user return
public_surface.detail: Agent's exit code from tty_proxy.run_with_proxy on success; 1 on: dry-run with invariant violations, non-macOS, euid 0, sandbox-exec missing, sandbox user missing, DarwinPackagesError, invariant failure, profile/stage/bootstrap install failure. sys.exit()ed by run_cmd.py.

public_surface.name: nix flake attrs (darwin)
public_surface.detail: `nix build --extra-experimental-features 'nix-command flakes' --impure --no-link --print-out-paths .#packages.aarch64-darwin.yoloDarwinPackages` (last stdout line = store path; <out>/bin → PATH prefix; <out>/lib/pkgconfig → PKG_CONFIG_PATH) and `nix eval --impure --json .#darwinUnavailablePackages.aarch64-darwin` (skip list, non-fatal on failure). cwd=repo_root; timeouts 1800s/120s.

public_surface.name: broker_socket_grant_commands
public_surface.detail: chgrp/chmod argv (parent 0750, socket 0660, group _yolojail) letting the sandbox uid reach the claude-oauth-broker's local unix socket directly — pure builder, wiring status unclear (no caller found in src outside this module).

internal_deps: cli.config (_normalize_blocked_tools, _merge_mise_tools, _resolve_env_sources, _effective_packages — private helpers imported inside functions)
cli.tty_proxy (run_with_proxy — final launch, forwards stdin for sudo password)

src/entrypoint package: staged by cp -R and imported by the GENERATED bootstrap script (generate_shims, generate_agent_launchers, generate_bashrc, generate_mise_config, generate_mcp_wrappers, configure_git, configure_jj, _load_agents, agent_configs.CONFIG_WRITERS, agent_registry.AGENTS, module attr WORKSPACE)

third_party_deps.used_for: typer.Exit(1) in the 4 CLI commands + registration
all user-facing output incl. --dry-run plan rendering with markup ([bold red] etc.)
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: lipgloss/fatih-color; exact dry-run text parity requires reproducing rich's rendering or defining parity on stripped text

side_effects: Creates/deletes macOS directory-services records (dscl/dseditgroup/createhomedir) and the /Users/_yolojail home
Sets random account password (openssl rand → sudo sh -c dscl -passwd, value only in env YOLO_SBPW)
Writes root-owned 0444 files under /var/yolo-jail (Seatbelt profile, bootstrap-<cname>.py) via sudo mkdir/tee/chmod; recursively copies src/entrypoint there every run
Applies/strips ACLs across whole workspace trees (find … chmod +a / -N)
Runs nix build/eval on the host (writes to /nix/store; no GC root — documented FUTURE gap)
Reads host `git config --get user.name/user.email` and TERM/COLORTERM
Bootstrap (as sandbox user) writes shims/agent configs/yolo-log into sandbox home
Launches interactive sudo (password prompts through the TTY proxy)
python_specifics: CODE GENERATION OF PYTHON: entrypoint_bootstrap_script emits a Python program using repr()/!r embedding of paths and json.dumps(json.dumps(agents)) double-encoding; it rebinds entrypoint import-time constants by setting os.environ BEFORE `import entrypoint`, does sys.path.insert to the staged dir, and monkeypatches `entrypoint.WORKSPACE = Path(...)` post-import. A Go port must still emit this exact Python text as long as src/entrypoint stays Python — the generated script is a cross-language contract, and Go must replicate Python repr() escaping of arbitrary paths exactly
Injectable-function test seams: resolve_python(exists=os.path.exists) — Go needs an fs interface
Tests monkeypatch subprocess.run and cli-module attributes (e.g. cli.macos_user.run_macos_user swapped in test_runtime.py) — Go needs an exec/runner abstraction for the same coverage
Deferred/inline imports (from .config import _private_helpers, import typer inside functions) to dodge import cycles and keep non-macOS import cheap
typer.Exit exception-based exit; dataclass RunPlan with field(default_factory=…)
getpass.getuser() fallback to $USER; os.geteuid() root refusal
_sh_quote (single-quote bash escaping) and _sbpl_str (SBPL double-quote escaping) are hand-rolled and byte-contractual

seams.name: runtime dispatch (whole-backend swap)
seams.contract: run_cmd.py:1257 `if runtime == 'macos-user': run_macos_user(workspace, config, agents, agent_argv, repo_src=…, dry_run=…) → sys.exit(rc)`. Entire backend behind one function taking (workspace path, parsed config dict, agents list, agent argv) and returning an exit code.
seams.swap_mechanism:
Replace the call with exec of a Go binary (e.g. `yolo-macos-user run --config --workspace

-- `), selected by env var YOLO_MACOS_IMPL=go; Python remains the CLI front.
seams.parity_test: `yolo run --dry-run` output: it renders EVERY artifact (sudo command list, full SBPL text, full bootstrap script text, launch argv, invariants) and runs on any OS with no side effects — diff Python vs Go dry-run output for a matrix of configs/workspaces. This is the designed-in parity gate.

seams.name: pure artifact builders (golden-text seam)
seams.contract: create_user_commands/delete_user_commands/shared_root_provision_commands (List[List[str]] argv), workspace_acl_aces (3 ACE strings), seatbelt_profile / fix_permissions_script / workspace_acl_strip_script / macos_log_wrapper_script / entrypoint_bootstrap_script (exact text), launch_argv/sandbox_path/_bootstrap_argv (argv), plan_invariants (message list)
seams.swap_mechanism: Port builders to Go first as a library + tiny CLI that prints each artifact for given inputs; Python can shell out to it, or run both and assert equality during transition.
seams.parity_test: Byte-for-byte golden comparison Python vs Go for identical inputs (incl. paths with quotes/backslashes to exercise _sh_quote/_sbpl_str/repr escaping). tests/test_macos_user.py (~60 tests) already encodes the contracts and translates directly.

seams.name: darwin_packages / nix boundary
seams.contract: Two subprocess invocations with fixed argv (build_profile_argv / unavailable_eval_argv), cwd=repo_root, env=os.environ±YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES(json); output contract: last non-empty stdout line = store path; skip list = JSON array; DarwinPackagesError semantics (nix missing / build fail / empty out → abort run; skip-list failure → non-fatal []).
seams.swap_mechanism: Standalone Go binary or package; process boundary is nix itself so Go is a drop-in. Flag/env-select `materialize` implementation in run_macos_user.
seams.parity_test: tests/test_darwin_packages.py (15 tests) port 1:1; plus compare DarwinPackages{path_prefix,env,skipped} JSON between implementations against a real flake on a Mac.

seams.name: macos-* setup commands
seams.contract: Each is `sudo <argv>` sequences from the pure builders + a handful of queries (id, dscl -list, openssl rand, git config). No sockets, no state beyond the OS itself.
seams.swap_mechanism: Individual Go subcommands registered in the Python typer app as passthrough execs (yolo macos-setup → exec yolo-macos setup).
seams.parity_test: Dry-run/echo mode printing the sudo command list; diff against Python's builders. Real-Mac smoke: run setup+teardown, assert dscl state identical.

existing: tests/test_macos_user.py (803 lines, ~60 tests): Seatbelt profile content/ordering/escaping, launch argv (env -i scrub, protected quartet, PATH order, darwin prefix position, cd-into-workspace), user create/delete cmd lists (hidden, de-staffed, no password in argv, 0750 home), ACE splits + batched fix-permissions script, bootstrap script (reused generators, Linux-only steps skipped, HOME rebind, yolo-log install), fail-closed off-macOS + refuses-root (run/setup/teardown), next_free_id, macos_log modes, broker socket grant, git-identity-only env, RunPlan invariants incl. darwin-PATH wiring guard

tests/test_darwin_packages.py (15 tests): argv shapes, YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES marshaling, profile_paths (bin-only, pkgconfig), locked rev vs real flake.lock, materialize success/nix-missing/build-fail/empty-out/skip-list-nonfatal via subprocess.run monkeypatch
tests/test_runtime.py: macos-user dispatch never touches podman; runtime_for_check on macOS
tests/test_cli_commands.py: yolo check skips container probe for macos-user; tests/test_config_merge.py: runtime validation accepts macos-user
gaps: ZERO real-macOS execution coverage — support matrix marks macos-user 🟡 'never tested on real macOS'; all orchestrator paths past the preconditions (sudo tee, dscl execution, sandbox-exec launch, tty_proxy interaction) are untested
--dry-run printed output has no golden test (rich markup) — the natural cross-impl parity artifact is unpinned
macos_setup/teardown/unshare/fix-permissions happy paths only tested for refusals, not command execution ordering
broker_socket_grant_commands has a unit test but no integration/caller
test_coverage.assessment: Excellent Linux-runnable unit coverage of every PURE builder (deliberate design: 'everything that produces an artifact is a pure function'), making a Go port highly test-drivable; but the impure macOS orchestration layer has no ground truth at all, so parity there can only be established on real Mac hardware.

porting_hazards: HIGHLY IN FLUX — port LAST or defer. docs/macos-backend-direction.md (2026-07-14) is 'Status: DECISION PENDING' on whether macos-user survives at all ('worried we bolted on an SV clone'); docs/macos.md currently states the backend 'was prototyped and then removed (recoverable at git tag macos-user-experiment)' while the code is fully present (revived per docs/handoff-macos-user-revive-plan.md) — docs and code actively disagree. Recent commits (refuse-sudo fix 6534519, AC container-builder proven on real HW 2026-07-17) show the macOS strategy tilting toward the container/Apple-Container path. Porting this module now risks porting dead code.

macos_shared_root config key is documented in docstrings and error messages but NOT wired into config.py (revive-plan item U4 pending) — the surface is incomplete; SHARED_ROOT_DEFAULT is effectively hardcoded today.

Generated-Python contract: entrypoint_bootstrap_script emits Python source that imports and monkeypatches the src/entrypoint package (env-before-import constant rebinding, entrypoint.WORKSPACE assignment, CONFIG_WRITERS/_load_agents loop). Go must emit byte-identical Python (repr() escaping, json.dumps(json.dumps()) nesting) until/unless entrypoint is ported — sequencing dependency on the entrypoint group.

Deep reach into cli.config PRIVATE helpers (_normalize_blocked_tools, _merge_mise_tools, _resolve_env_sources, _effective_packages) — a Go swap of this module alone needs those semantics duplicated or an IPC/CLI shim; note _resolve_env_sources failures are swallowed (bare except) — replicate that leniency.

Exact-text contracts everywhere: _sh_quote single-quote escaping, _sbpl_str SBPL escaping, SBPL last-match-wins ordering, ACE right-lists, bash script bodies, PATH join order — a one-character drift changes security posture; use golden tests.
Interactive sudo through tty_proxy (stdin-forwarded password prompts, --login zsh, controlling TTY preservation via sudo -u not launchctl asuser) — TTY semantics must be preserved by any Go launcher.
--dry-run exit-code contract (0 clean / 1 violations) and its rich-markup output are the de facto parity surface but aren't golden-pinned; pin them before porting.

run_macos_user’s ordering is behavioral: dry-run returns BEFORE the macOS/root gates (so it works on Linux CI); cheap preconditions before the up-to-30-min nix build; plan built only after gates (it reads host git config). Reordering breaks tests and UX.
nix store path has no GC root (documented FUTURE review #12) — replicate the gap, don't 'fix' it during a no-functionality-change port.
macos_user.py truncation trap for future readers: file is 1664 lines; orchestrator + setup commands live past line 1255.
notes: Files: /workspace/src/cli/macos_user.py, /workspace/src/cli/darwin_packages.py; tests /workspace/tests/test_macos_user.py, /workspace/tests/test_darwin_packages.py; flake attrs at /workspace/flake.nix:784-800; dispatch /workspace/src/cli/run_cmd.py:1252-1277; command registration /workspace/src/cli/__init__.py:383-397; check integration /workspace/src/cli/check_cmd.py:898. The module was DESIGNED for exactly this kind of port: everything observable is a pure data-returning function with Linux unit tests, and --dry-run renders the full artifact set — so mechanical portability is high. The real risk is strategic, not technical: the backend's existence is under an explicit pending decision and docs already describe it as removed, while the container/Apple-Container path (separate module group) is where verified-on-real-HW momentum is. Recommend sequencing: port the container backend groups first; revisit cli-macos only after the direction decision lands. If ported, do darwin_packages first (small, stable nix boundary), then the pure builders with golden tests, and keep the generated-Python bootstrap emission until the entrypoint group is ported.
